headline: WebAuthn attestation verification covers Ed448 and the RFC 9864 fully-specified COSE algorithms, and hardens credential-key conformance.
summary: pki.webauthn now evaluates a WebAuthn credential public key and attestation signature across the full W3C WebAuthn (Level 3) algorithm set. It gains the RFC 9864 fully-specified COSE algorithm identifiers a relying party may receive -- ESP256 (-9), ESP384 (-51), ESP512 (-52), Ed25519 (-19), and Ed448 (-53) -- so an assertion or attestation signed under any of them verifies instead of erroring. Ed448 credentials, in particular, were previously unsupported: -53 is the only WebAuthn path to Ed448 (the legacy EdDSA identifier -8 is Ed25519 only), so an Ed448 authenticator could not be verified before. Alongside the wider coverage, the credential-key checks are stricter, matching the WebAuthn conformance requirements: alg -8 (EdDSA) now requires the Ed25519 curve and rejects a mismatched curve; an EC2 credential key must use the uncompressed point form (a compressed sign-bit y is rejected); the public-key point is validated on its curve so an off-curve or identity point fails closed; and an ECDSA attestation signature must be a minimally-encoded DER ECDSA-Sig-Value (a non-minimal, negative, zero, or over-size r/s is rejected rather than normalized-and-accepted). The whole verifier is validated against the official W3C WebAuthn Level 3 test-vector suite -- every published vector across ES256 / ES384 / ES512 / RS256 / Ed25519 / Ed448 and the packed / tpm / apple / fido-u2f / none formats -- in addition to the existing captured-attestation oracle. Fail-closed as before: every malformed shape or failed check throws a typed WebauthnError (webauthn/*), never a partial verdict.

Added: pki.webauthn now verifies the RFC 9864 fully-specified COSE algorithm identifiers a WebAuthn relying party may receive: ESP256 (-9), ESP384 (-51), ESP512 (-52), Ed25519 (-19), and Ed448 (-53). Ed448 (-53) is the only WebAuthn path to Ed448, so an Ed448 credential now verifies rather than erroring as an unsupported algorithm.

The verifier is now checked against the official W3C WebAuthn Level 3 test-vector suite -- every published vector across ES256 / ES384 / ES512 / RS256 / Ed25519 / Ed448 and the packed / self / tpm / apple / fido-u2f / none formats -- as an independent cross-implementation oracle.
Changed: A WebAuthn credential key with COSE alg -8 (EdDSA) now requires curve Ed25519 (crv 6), matching the WebAuthn algorithm-identifier profile; an -8 key claiming Ed448 (crv 7) is rejected -- Ed448 is carried under the fully-specified identifier -53 instead.
An EC2 credential key must use the uncompressed point form; the compressed sign-bit y encoding is rejected for WebAuthn credential keys.
Fixed: The WebAuthn credential public-key point is now validated on its curve, so an off-curve or identity EC/Edwards point fails closed at decode rather than being carried into a later verify step.

An ECDSA attestation signature is now enforced as a minimally-encoded DER ECDSA-Sig-Value (X.690): a non-minimal, negative, zero, or over-size r/s coordinate is rejected as malformed instead of being stripped and accepted.
